opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My head feels like it's going to explode, the light is burning my eyes, and I can't stop vomiting.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: I've had this migraine for two days, my usual tablet came straight back up, and I can't keep anything down — I need help tonight.

symptom_narrative.onset: it started about two days ago with the visual warning signs I sometimes get
symptom_narrative.duration: around 48 hours and it just won't lift
symptom_narrative.character: intense throbbing on one side of my head, near my temple
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst it's been in a long time — I can't function at all
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant now, it hasn't eased at all
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: only lying in a completely dark, silent room helps a little
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: any light or noise, and moving around makes the nausea worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: light is unbearable, I keep vomiting, and my thinking feels really slow and foggy
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I get migraines a lot but this one won't stop like the others usually do
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm stuck in bed, I can't keep water or my tablets down

past_medical_history.how_managed: triptan tablets and lying in the dark

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: 50mg, took one about 24 hours ago but vomited it straight back up

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: my partner is here with me and is really worried about how slow and out of it I seem

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading in a dark room while unwell
likely elevated by pain and dehydration
